description: Add DigitalOcean as a sandbox provider for hal sandbox, alongside the existing Hetzner provider. Implements the Provider interface by shelling out to doctl, registers in the provider factory, and adds CLI setup prompts.

userStories.title: DigitalOcean provider implementation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a DigitalOceanProvider struct in internal/sandbox/provider_digitalocean.go that implements the Provider interface by shelling out to doctl, so that all sandbox operations work against DigitalOcean droplets.
acceptanceCriteria: provider_digitalocean.go exists in internal/sandbox/ and defines a DigitalOceanProvider struct

Create runs doctl compute droplet create with --size, --image ubuntu-24-04-x64, --ssh-keys, --user-data-file, and --wait flags, then retrieves the public IP via doctl compute droplet get with --format PublicIPv4 --no-header
Stop runs doctl compute droplet-action shutdown with the droplet ID
Delete runs doctl compute droplet delete with --force flag
Status runs doctl compute droplet get with --format ID,Name,Status,PublicIPv4 and returns structured output
SSH execs into ssh root@<ip>
Exec runs ssh root@<ip> -- <args...>
If doctl is not found on PATH, every method returns an error containing 'doctl not found' with install/auth instructions
If a doctl command exits non-zero, the stderr output is wrapped into the returned error
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Register provider in factory
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the ProviderFromConfig factory in internal/sandbox/provider.go to recognize 'digitalocean' and return a DigitalOceanProvider, so the rest of the codebase can use it without knowing the concrete type.
acceptanceCriteria: ProviderFromConfig returns a DigitalOceanProvider when config has sandbox.provider set to digitalocean
The provider is constructed with the sshKey and size values read from sandbox.digitalocean.* config fields
An unrecognized provider string still returns the existing unsupported provider error
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: CLI setup prompt for DigitalOcean
userStories.description: As a user, I want hal sandbox setup to offer DigitalOcean as a provider choice and collect my SSH key fingerprint and droplet size, so I can configure DO sandboxes interactively.
acceptanceCriteria: The provider selection prompt includes a DigitalOcean option alongside existing providers
When DigitalOcean is selected, the user is prompted for an SSH key fingerprint with hint: doctl compute ssh-key list
The user is prompted for a droplet size slug with default s-2vcpu-4gb
Selecting DigitalOcean when doctl is not on PATH prints an error telling the user to install and authenticate doctl, and aborts setup with no partial config saved
Config is saved with sandbox.provider: digitalocean, sandbox.digitalocean.sshKey: <fingerprint>, sandbox.digitalocean.size: <slug>
Existing provider choices and their setup flows are unchanged
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Unit tests for provider and config
userStories.description: As a developer, I want unit tests covering the DigitalOcean provider's command assembly, config parsing, and error paths, so regressions are caught without requiring a real DO account.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests exist in internal/sandbox/provider_digitalocean_test.go
Create test verifies the assembled doctl command args include --size, --image ubuntu-24-04-x64, --ssh-keys, --user-data-file, and --wait
Delete test verifies --force flag is present in assembled args
Stop test verifies the droplet-action shutdown command is assembled with the correct droplet identifier
Status test verifies --format ID,Name,Status,PublicIPv4 is present in assembled args
A test verifies that when doctl is not on PATH, the provider returns an error containing 'doctl not found'
A test verifies ProviderFromConfig returns a DigitalOceanProvider for provider: digitalocean config and reads sshKey/size fields correctly
All exec.Command calls are mocked with no real doctl invocations
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
